name: Cruzan by Cruzan v. Director, Missouri Department of Health
question: Did the Due Process Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment permit Cruzan's parents to refuse life-sustaining treatment on their daughter's behalf?
description: A case in which the Court held that a Missouri state hospital had the right to keep a patient in a vegetative state alive, despite the wishes of the patient's parents, due to a lack of otherwise "clear and convincing" wishes on the part of the patient.
